info.title: Jito Block Engine — bundles + transactions (drafted from docs/source)
info.description:
Gecko's comprehension surface for the Jito Block Engine (https://docs.jito.wtf), the JSON-RPC service Solana searchers/dApps use to submit MEV bundles and low-latency transactions. Built by Jito Labs.

NO OpenAPI ships for Jito: this spec is the docs->draft-OpenAPI on-ramp. Method names, endpoint paths, and parameters were read from the rendered docs page https://docs.jito.wtf/lowlatencytxnsend/ (a JS-driven Sphinx/RTD site, so a headless browser was needed to see the request/response tables) and cross-checked against the official SDK source in jito-labs/jito-py-rpc (jito_py_rpc/jito_jsonrpc_sdk.py), jito-labs/jito-go-rpc, jito-labs/jito-js-rpc, jito-labs/jito-rust-rpc, and jito-labs/mev-protos (json_rpc/http.md).

JSON-RPC<->OpenAPI MISMATCH (handled, not hidden): the Block Engine is JSON-RPC over HTTP POST, not REST, and it is NOT one-endpoint-per-method — several methods share a wire path (e.g. sendBundle and getTipAccounts both POST /api/v1/bundles; the method lives in the envelope). OpenAPI requires a unique (path, method) per operation, so each JSON-RPC method is surfaced here as its OWN question-shaped operation: operationId = the exact JSON-RPC method, path = a virtual /{method} route, requestBody = that method's params BY NAME. The true wire path and envelope method are carried per-operation in x-jsonrpc-endpoint / x-jsonrpc-method, and the positional order Jito expects in `params` is carried in x-jsonrpc-params.

HONESTY: Jito's params are POSITIONAL arrays (e.g. sendBundle params = [[transactions], {encoding}]). We model them by name for first-call-correct discovery and record the positional order in x-jsonrpc-params. A LIVE caller assembles the JSON-RPC envelope {jsonrpc:"2.0", id, method: <x-jsonrpc-method>, params: <named fields in x-jsonrpc-params order>} and POSTs to x-jsonrpc-endpoint. This recorded/comprehension demo surfaces each method directly so an agent can find and shape the call correctly first try.

AUTH: default sends now need NO auth key (docs: 'You no longer need an approved auth key for default sends'; use Null/None). An OPTIONAL UUID unlocks higher rate limits, sent as the `x-jito-auth` header or a `?uuid=` query param — modeled as the optional `jitoUuidAuth` scheme (every op accepts no-auth OR the UUID). getTipFloor lives on a DIFFERENT host (bundles.jito.wtf) and is plain REST, not JSON-RPC — noted on that operation.

x-jsonrpc.note: Virtual /{method} paths exist only so OpenAPI can give each JSON-RPC method its own operation. Live transport collapses them to a POST at x-jsonrpc-endpoint with the method in the envelope. `params` is a POSITIONAL array; assemble it from the named requestBody fields in the order given by x-jsonrpc-params.

servers.description: Mainnet Block Engine (global). Regional hosts exist (amsterdam./frankfurt./ny./tokyo. etc. .mainnet.block-engine.jito.wtf) with identical paths.

jitoUuidAuth.name: x-jito-auth
jitoUuidAuth.description: OPTIONAL UUID for higher rate limits. Default sends need no auth. May instead be passed as a `?uuid=<uuid>` query param. Injected by the access layer, never surfaced to the agent.

TxEncoding.description: Encoding of the transaction data. base64 recommended; base58 is slow and DEPRECATED.

RpcSendResult.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope with a single string result (a signature or bundle id).

TipAccountsResult.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope; result is the list of tip accounts (base-58 pubkeys).

BundleStatusesResult.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope; result carries the confirmed on-chain status of each queried bundle. null result if a bundle is not found.

transactions.description: Base-58 encoded signatures applied by the bundle.

err.description: Any retryable/non-retryable error; retry if retryable.

InflightBundleStatusesResult.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope; result carries the in-flight status (last 5 minutes) of each queried bundle.

status.description: Invalid: not in system (5-min lookback). Pending: not failed/landed/invalid. Failed: all regions marked failed, not forwarded. Landed: on-chain.

landed_slot.description: Slot the bundle landed in, else null if not Landed.

TipFloor.description: Recent Jito tip statistics (SOL). Plain REST array, NOT a JSON-RPC envelope.

post.summary: Submit a bundle of up to 5 signed transactions to land atomically (all-or-nothing).
post.description: JSON-RPC `sendBundle`. Relays a client-built bundle to the leader unmodified; returns a bundle_id immediately (receipt only — not a landing guarantee; poll getBundleStatuses). A tip to one of the getTipAccounts accounts is required to be considered; min tip 1000 lamports. Wire: POST /api/v1/bundles, envelope params = [[transactions], {encoding}].

transactions.description: Fully-signed transactions (max 5), base64 (recommended) or base58 (DEPRECATED) encoded strings. Executed sequentially and atomically.

200.description: Bundle received; result is the bundle_id (SHA-256 of the bundle's tx signatures).

post.summary: Get the 8 tip accounts a bundle must pay to be considered in the auction.
post.description: JSON-RPC `getTipAccounts`. Returns the designated tip accounts (constant set of 8). Pick one at random to reduce contention, then transfer SOL to it as your bundle tip. Wire: POST /api/v1/bundles, envelope params = [] (no params). DRIFT NOTE: the docs `curl` example posts to /api/v1/getTipAccounts, but the official SDKs (jito-py-rpc/jito-go-rpc) post to /api/v1/bundles; JSON-RPC dispatches on the envelope `method`, so both resolve — we carry the SDK (executable) path.

200.description: The list of tip accounts (base-58 pubkeys).

post.summary: Get the confirmed on-chain status of submitted bundles by bundle_id.
post.description: JSON-RPC `getBundleStatuses` (like getSignatureStatuses). Returns null for an unknown/unlanded bundle, else context.slot plus each bundle's transactions, slot, confirmation_status and err. Searches recent history only (up to ~300 rooted slots). Wire: POST /api/v1/getBundleStatuses, envelope params = [[bundleIds]].

200.description: Confirmed status per bundle (null result if not found).

post.summary: Get the in-flight status (last 5 minutes) of submitted bundles by bundle_id.
post.description: JSON-RPC `getInflightBundleStatuses`. For each bundle_id (max 5) returns status one of Invalid/Pending/Failed/Landed plus landed_slot. Faster liveness check than getBundleStatuses over a 5-minute window. Wire: POST /api/v1/getInflightBundleStatuses, envelope params = [[bundleIds]].

post.summary: Send a single signed transaction through Jito with MEV protection (fast landing).
post.description: JSON-RPC `sendTransaction`. A direct proxy to Solana's sendTransaction that forwards to the validator with MEV protection; always sets skip_preflight=true. Set query bundleOnly=true for revert protection (sent as a single-tx bundle). Min tip 1000 lamports; a 70/30 priority-fee/Jito-tip split is recommended. Wire: POST /api/v1/transactions, envelope params = [transaction, {encoding}].

parameters.name: bundleOnly
parameters.description: If true, enable revert protection by sending the transaction exclusively as a single-transaction bundle.

transaction.description: The fully-signed transaction (first signature embedded), base64 (recommended) or base58 (slow, DEPRECATED) encoded.

200.description: result is the transaction signature (as Solana sendTransaction). bundle_id is returned in the x-bundle-id response header if sent as a bundle.

get.summary: Get recent Jito tip amounts (percentiles) to size a competitive tip.
get.description: Plain REST GET (NOT JSON-RPC, and on a DIFFERENT host). Returns recent landed-tip percentiles in SOL so you can price a bundle/transaction tip. True URL: GET https://bundles.jito.wtf/api/v1/bundles/tip_floor. A live WebSocket stream also exists at wss://bundles.jito.wtf/api/v1/bundles/tip_stream (not modeled here).
